title: potentilla arenaria subvar. parceglandulosa
intro: Potentilla arenaria subvar. parceglandulosa, also known as the coastal cinquefoil, is a subspecies of the Potentilla arenaria plant, a perennial herb native to coastal areas of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Potentilla arenaria subvar. parceglandulosa can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
